metaTitle: Keyboard Shortcuts — Work Faster in Tablesmit
metaDescription: Every Tablesmit action has a keyboard shortcut. Navigate cells, add rows, find and replace, and export to PDF — all without leaving the keyboard.
heroHeadline: Your hands never have to leave the keyboard.
heroSubtext: Every meaningful action in Tablesmit has a shortcut. Navigate, edit, export, and search at the speed you actually think — no mouse required.

shortcuts.description: Move through your table precisely without reaching for the mouse.

items.label: Move between cells
items.note: Arrow key navigation works when a cell is selected but not in edit mode.

items.label: Enter edit mode
items.note: Press Enter on a focused cell to start typing into it.

items.label: Exit edit mode
items.note: Cancels the current edit without saving the change.

shortcuts.description: Modify cell content and correct mistakes without interrupting your flow.

items.label: Undo last action
items.note: Up to 50 actions deep. The tooltip on the Undo button shows how many actions are available.

items.label: Delete cell content
items.note: Clears the content of the selected cell without removing the cell itself.

shortcuts.description: Add rows and columns without breaking your focus from the content.

items.label: Add row
items.note: Appends a new empty row to the bottom of the table.

items.label: Add column
items.note: Appends a new empty column to the right of the table.

shortcuts.description: Find content across all cells and replace it without using the mouse.

items.label: Find
items.note: Opens the find panel. Highlights all matching cells as you type.

items.label: Find and replace
items.note: Opens the find and replace panel. Replace one match or all at once.

shortcuts.description: Get your table out of the browser and into the world — instantly.

items.label: Export to PDF
items.note: Exports the table as a clean PDF — no browser print dialog, no UI chrome.

items.label: Export to Excel
items.note: Downloads a genuine .xlsx file with merged cells and formatting preserved.

items.label: Copy as image
items.note: Renders the table to a PNG and writes it to your clipboard. Paste directly into any document, presentation, or design tool.

items.label: Toggle keyboard shortcuts
items.note: Opens or closes this shortcuts reference. Press it again to dismiss.

benefits.heading: Stay in flow
benefits.body: Every time you reach for the mouse, you lose a beat. Keyboard shortcuts keep your hands where your thinking is — on the content, not the interface.

benefits.heading: Navigate large tables precisely
benefits.body: Tab, Shift+Tab, and Arrow keys make moving through a 50-column table a matter of keystrokes. No clicking, no scrolling to find the next cell.

benefits.heading: Export without breaking focus
benefits.body: Ctrl+P and Ctrl+E let you export to PDF or Excel in the middle of editing — without opening a menu, without taking your hands off the keyboard.

benefits.heading: Undo confidently
benefits.body: Ctrl+Z goes up to 50 actions deep. Edit freely, knowing you can always reverse the last step without hunting for the Undo button.

steps.heading: Learn the navigation shortcuts first
steps.body: Tab and Shift+Tab are the most important. They let you move through a table like a spreadsheet — left to right, row by row. Arrow keys give you full directional control when not in edit mode.

steps.heading: Use Ctrl+L and Ctrl+R to build structure
steps.body: Add rows with Ctrl+L and columns with Ctrl+R while filling in data. You never need to stop typing to extend the table.

steps.heading: Find and correct with Ctrl+F and Ctrl+H
steps.body: Made a spelling error across 20 rows? Ctrl+H opens find and replace. Fix it once and replace all. Done in seconds.

steps.heading: Export directly from the keyboard
steps.body: When your table is ready, Ctrl+P sends it to PDF and Ctrl+E sends it to Excel. No toolbar required. Press Ctrl+/ at any time to see the full reference.

useCases: Researchers filling in large data tables without switching between mouse and keyboard
Writers who want to build and export a table without interrupting their typing rhythm
Analysts who move quickly between cells using Tab navigation during data entry
Anyone who wants to export a finished table instantly with a single keypress

_codex_notes.schema_extension: This page uses a 'shortcuts' field not present in the base FeaturePage type. Extend the type as shown below and handle it in FeatureDetailPage.
_codex_notes.rendering: In FeatureDetailPage: if (page.shortcuts) render a ShortcutsTable component between the hero and benefits sections. The table renders each category as a group with a heading, then each item as a row: label on the left, key badge(s) on the right. 'coming-soon' items render with an amber 'Coming soon' badge next to the key. Key badges: font-mono text-xs bg-surface border border-border rounded-sm px-2 py-1. Coming soon badge: same as accent-light with amber text.
_codex_notes.coming_soon_item: Ctrl+C (Copy as image) is in the pipeline. It is intentionally listed with status: 'coming-soon' to signal intent without shipping broken functionality. Remove the status field when the feature ships.
